title: feat(mysql): Support `CHANGE COLUMN`.
body:
In MySQL, `CHANGE COLUMN` will redefine a column, optionally renaming it:

```
CHANGE [COLUMN] old_col_name new_col_name column_definition
    [FIRST | AFTER col_name]
```

When factoring this in, I evaluated two options:

* Create a separate `ChangeColumn` type similar to the `ModifyColumn` type. Given that the `MODIFY` and `CHANGE` syntax are nearly identical except for the ability to rename the column, this seemed like the wrong approach.

* Add a `rename_from` attribute to the `ModifyColumn` implementation, accepting the "original" column name.

Even though `ChangeColumn` seemed to be a more orthogonal change with cleaner code, I chose the latter because it seemed more semantically correct: apart from a `new_col_name`, they share the same syntax and behaviour.
